subject: Geography
title: The Caribbean Arc
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Caribbean Arc.

hook.text: The Caribbean islands curve in an arc from Cuba to Trinidad, tracing a tectonic plate edge.

buildup.text: The Atlantic plate dives under the Caribbean plate, fueling volcanic eruptions along the arc.

discovery.text: In 1902, Mount Pelée erupted and destroyed the city of Saint-Pierre, killing 30,000 in minutes.

twist.text: The same forces that create Caribbean volcanoes also produce its beautiful white sand beaches.

climax.text: The arc is one of the most hurricane-prone regions on Earth due to warm ocean currents.

punchline.text: Paradise islands born from violent geology.
